intro: Oreocarya subcapitata, also known as Desert Bluebells, is a perennial plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may aid in reducing inflammation and protecting against oxidative stress. Bioactive constituents of Oreocarya subcapitata include alkaloids, phenolic acids, and flavonoids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. Desert Bluebells are often used to prepare herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are commonly used to support immune function and alleviate respiratory issues.
